text:
# Final call for Agribusiness Award - innovations deserve a podium

[Entrepreneurial story](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/ondernemershalen/index.html)

[Agricultural](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/agrarisch/index.html)

11/19/2025 • 2 minutes reading

[Pierre Berntsen](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/autoriteit/pierre-berntsen/index.html)

Melanie Murk

Until December 1, it is still possible to register your innovative product, service or chain concept for the Agribusiness Award. Complete the registration form today.

> “By participating, you are forced to look at your own innovations through the eyes of someone else.”

Laurens Sloot

Jury chairman Agribusiness Awards, Director and professor of Retail & Entrepreneurship

EFMI Business School and University of Groningen

In an earlier interview with [Boerenbusiness](https://www.boerenbusiness.nl/artikelen/10914505/kijk-door-ogen-van-ander-naar-je-eigen-informatie) jury chairman Laurens Sloot encouraged companies in the agri and food sector to participate. "It broadens your network and a strong network promotes innovation." The director of EFMI Business School and part-time professor of Retail & Entrepreneurship at the University of Groningen joined the jury of the [Agribusiness Award](http://agribusinessaward.nl/) last year.

## Strong revenue model increases success

Companies that want to register their innovation for the Agribusiness Award 2026 must take a number of [conditions] into account (http://agribusinessaward.nl/regulations). As jury chairman, Sloot mainly looks at how innovative and distinctive the innovation is. But also whether it is mainly an idea or a concept or whether the innovation has already proven itself. "And we look at whether there is a potentially strong revenue model. Something will only fly if companies can actually earn something from it." The reason for Sloot to contribute to the Agribusiness Award is the impact that the election makes.

> “With the great visibility of the Agribusiness Award, it is not only a compliment to the winning company, but also a calling card for the sector as a whole.”

metadata.source_title: Final call for Agribusiness Award - innovations deserve a podium

text:
> “With the great visibility of the Agribusiness Award, it is not only a compliment to the winning company, but also a calling card for the sector as a whole.”

Laurens Sloot

Jury chairman Agribusiness Awards, Director and professor of Retail & Entrepreneurship

EFMI Business School and University of Groningen

## Strong network promotes innovative capacity

His advice to companies that want to register their innovation: "Dare to open up. By participating in the Agribusiness Award you are forced to look at your own innovations through the eyes of someone else. You also broaden your network and new things will automatically come your way. People like to think along and a strong network promotes innovative capacity."

## Register before December 1

Do you have an innovative product, service or chain concept that deserves a podium? Then register for the Agribusiness Award 2026. Registration closes on December 1, 2025, so go to [www.agribusinessaward.nl](http://www.agribusinessaward.nl/) and complete the registration form.

## Read more stories from entrepreneurs in the agricultural sector

Are you considering starting your own business, are you on the edge of the start-up process or would you like to take a look behind the scenes of someone else? Dive into our agricultural entrepreneurial stories and get inspiration and tips from the experiences that entrepreneurs share.

[More entrepreneurial stories in the agricultural sector](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/ondernemershistories/agrarisch/index.html "Entrepreneurial stories in the agricultural sector")
